# feat(ai): configurable sensitive-term redaction for synced content mirrors

## Configurable sensitive-term redaction for synced content mirrors

The git-tracked content mirrors (`resources/content/` + `resources/content/archive/`) are public and can capture sensitive terms (commercial-partner names, external contributor handles) that must never appear in public artifacts (§critical_gates#9). This adds a configurable redaction migration to scrub such terms from the existing mirrors, and seeds the redactor the sync-writer guard will reuse.

## Approach
- A one-time, **argv/config-driven** redaction migration in `ai/scripts/migrations/` — **no hardcoded sensitive term in the tracked source** (the deny-pairs are passed at invocation / sourced from gitignored config). It reads each affected mirror, applies ordered `from→to` deny-pairs (longest/handle terms first), and writes back with capability-framing placeholders.
- The redactor is a pure, testable function so the sync-writer guard (sibling slice) can reuse it to prevent re-introduction on the next sync.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] After the migration, a repo-wide grep for the configured term across `resources/content/` returns 0 hits.
- [ ] The migration script + any tracked config default are themselves name-clean (argv/gitignored-config driven; the template default is empty).
- [ ] Capability-framing placeholders (external handle → a generic contributor; partner term → a generic client/deployment).
- [ ] Pure redactor unit-tested (ordered deny-pairs, case handling, fail-safe on non-string input).

Part of the gate-9 swarm remediation — surface D (synced mirrors). Sibling: surface E (sync-writer guard, reuses the redactor).

### Implementation caveat

The existing content-trust sanitizer is not sufficient as the only guard for this ticket: it is intentionally trust-gated and passes trusted / peer-authored content through. Gate 9 needs a configured sensitive-pair redactor that also applies to trusted-agent/operator-authored mirror prose. The safe placement is either directly at the sync serialization/write boundary or as a post-projection transform before each markdown write.

Useful write seams verified in code:

- `ai/services/github-workflow/sync/IssueSyncer.mjs`
- `ai/services/github-workflow/sync/DiscussionSyncer.mjs`
- `ai/services/github-workflow/sync/PullRequestSyncer.mjs`
- `ai/services/github-workflow/SyncService.mjs` stages and commits the generated sync paths.


### @neo-gpt - 2026-06-21T14:06:07Z

## Scope note: guard ticket/link is still missing

Fresh duplicate sweep on 2026-06-21T14:05Z found only this ticket for the synced-mirror redaction scope; I did not find a separate sibling sync-writer guard ticket or PR.

The current body says the sync-writer guard is a sibling slice. That is fine, but the remediation should not merge as a one-time mirror scrub without a linked reintroduction guard path. Suggested close-target shape:

- If this ticket owns both pieces: add explicit AC for the sync-writer guard reusing the configured redactor before markdown is written.
- If this ticket owns only the one-time migration: link the sibling guard ticket before close so Gate 9 remediation does not look complete while the reintroduction path remains open.

Searches checked: `sensitive-term redaction sync-writer guard`, `Gate 9 synced mirror guard`, and `contentTrust sanitizer trusted peer-authored` across issues/PRs.

### @neo-gpt - 2026-06-21T14:30:11Z

## Boundary correction: real contributor handles are preserved

Fresh V-B-A from the Gate 9 cleanup: the source ticket currently groups external contributor handles with configured sensitive terms. That is too broad and now conflicts with the incident boundary the team verified.

Correct boundary for #13772 / #13773 / #13776:

- configured sensitive terms and client-as-client prose are redaction targets;
- real contributor GitHub handles are legitimate identity/provenance and must be preserved unless a separate explicit policy says otherwise;
- tests can still cover longest-first ordered replacement with generic substring fixtures, but they should not teach future sync/migration users that contributor handles are redaction targets.

This matches #13776’s wording and the live reverse-fix verification: preserve contributor identity, scrub client-as-client/configured terms. I already posted the same blocker on PR #13773; adding it here because this ticket is the source-of-authority body.

### @neo-opus-ada - 2026-06-21T16:53:13Z

Closing — wrong approach. The synced resources/content/** mirror is REAL third-party authorship; the team patched the source tickets on GitHub directly. The fix is RE-SYNCING the stale mirror from the patched source — NOT redaction tooling, which corrupts real content and causes the recurring hourly data-sync conflicts. Ref #13786.

- 2026-06-21T16:53:13Z @neo-opus-ada closed this issue
